verses.latin_vulgate: Congregavitque Josue omnes tribus Israël in Sichem, et vocavit majores natu, ac principes, et judices, et magistros : steteruntque in conspectu Domini,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֶּֽאֱסֹ֧ף יְהוֹשֻׁ֛עַ אֶת־כׇּל־שִׁבְטֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל שְׁכֶ֑מָה וַיִּקְרָא֩ לְזִקְנֵ֨י יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל וּלְרָאשָׁ֗יו וּלְשֹֽׁפְטָיו֙ וּלְשֹׁ֣טְרָ֔יו וַיִּֽתְיַצְּב֖וּ לִפְנֵ֥י הָאֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joshua gathered together all the tribes of Israel in Sichem, and called for the ancients, and the princes and the judges, and the masters: and they stood in the sight of the Lord:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josua kokosi kaikki Israelin sukukunnat Sikemiin, ja kutsui edes vanhimmat Israelissa, päämiehet, tuomarit ja esimiehet; ja kuin he olivat seisatetut Jumalan eteen,
verses.king_james: And Joshua gathered all the tribes of Israel to Shechem, and called for the elders of Israel, and for their heads, and for their judges, and for their officers; and they presented themselves before God.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ad populum sic locutus est : Hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël : Trans fluvium habitaverunt patres vestri ab initio, Thare pater Abraham et Nachor : servieruntque diis alienis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁ֜עַ אֶל־כׇּל־הָעָ֗ם כֹּה־אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָה֮ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵל֒ בְּעֵ֣בֶר הַנָּהָ֗ר יָשְׁב֤וּ אֲבֽוֹתֵיכֶם֙ מֵֽעוֹלָ֔ם תֶּ֛רַח אֲבִ֥י אַבְרָהָ֖ם וַאֲבִ֣י נָח֑וֹר וַיַּעַבְד֖וּ אֱלֹהִ֥ים אֲחֵרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he spoke thus to the people: Thus saith the Lord the God of Israel: Your fathers dwelt of old on the other side of the river, Thare the father of Abraham, and Nachor: and they served strange gods.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sanoi Josua kaikelle kansalle: näin sanoo Herra Israelin Jumala: teidän isänne asuivat entiseen aikaan sillä puolella virtaa, Tara, Abrahamin ja Nahorin isä, ja palvelivat muita jumalia.
verses.king_james: And Joshua said unto all the people, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Your fathers dwelt on the other side of the flood in old time, even Terah, the father of Abraham, and the father of Nachor: and they served other gods.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tuli ergo patrem vestrum Abraham de Mesopotamiæ finibus, et adduxi eum in terram Chanaan, multiplicavique semen ejus,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָ֠אֶקַּ֠ח אֶת־אֲבִיכֶ֤ם אֶת־אַבְרָהָם֙ מֵעֵ֣בֶר הַנָּהָ֔ר וָֽאוֹלֵ֥ךְ אֹת֖וֹ בְּכׇל־אֶ֣רֶץ כְּנָ֑עַן (וארב) [וָֽאַרְבֶּה֙] אֶת־זַרְע֔וֹ וָאֶתֶּן־ל֖וֹ אֶת־יִצְחָֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I took your father Abraham from the borders of Mesopotamia: and brought him into the land of Chanaan: and I multiplied his seed,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin otin minä isänne Abrahamin tuolta puolelta virtaa, ja annoin hänen vaeltaa koko Kanaanin maalla, ja enensin hänen siemenensä, ja annoin hänelle Isaakin.
verses.king_james: And I took your father Abraham from the other side of the flood, and led him throughout all the land of Canaan, and multiplied his seed, and gave him Isaac.

verses.latin_vulgate: et dedi ei Isaac : illique rursum dedi Jacob et Esau. E quibus, Esau dedi montem Seir ad possidendum : Jacob vero et filii ejus descenderunt in Ægyptum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶתֵּ֣ן לְיִצְחָ֔ק אֶֽת־יַעֲקֹ֖ב וְאֶת־עֵשָׂ֑ו וָֽאֶתֵּ֨ן לְעֵשָׂ֜ו אֶת־הַ֤ר שֵׂעִיר֙ לָרֶ֣שֶׁת אוֹת֔וֹ וְיַעֲקֹ֥ב וּבָנָ֖יו יָרְד֥וּ מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And gave him Isaac: and to him again I gave Jacob and Esau. And I gave to Esau mount Seir for his possession: but Jacob and his children went down into Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Isaakille annoin minä Jakobin ja Esaun; ja annoin Esaulle Seirin vuoren asuaksensa. Mutta Jakob ja hänen lapsensa menivät alas Egyptin maalle.
verses.king_james: And I gave unto Isaac Jacob and Esau: and I gave unto Esau mount Seir, to possess it; but Jacob and his children went down into Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misique Moysen et Aaron, et percussi Ægyptum multis signis atque portentis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶשְׁלַ֞ח אֶת־מֹשֶׁ֤ה וְאֶֽת־אַהֲרֹן֙ וָאֶגֹּ֣ף אֶת־מִצְרַ֔יִם כַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשִׂ֖יתִי בְּקִרְבּ֑וֹ וְאַחַ֖ר הוֹצֵ֥אתִי אֶתְכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I sent Moses and Aaron, and I struck Egypt with many signs and wonders.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä lähetin Moseksen ja Aaronin, ja rankaisin Egyptin, niinkuin minä heidän seassansa tehnyt olen; sitte vein minä teidät ulos.
verses.king_james: I sent Moses also and Aaron, and I plagued Egypt, according to that which I did among them: and afterward I brought you out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eduxique vos et patres vestros de Ægypto, et venistis ad mare : persecutique sunt Ægyptii patres vestros cum curribus et equitatu, usque ad mare Rubrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאוֹצִ֤יא אֶת־אֲבֽוֹתֵיכֶם֙ מִמִּצְרַ֔יִם וַתָּבֹ֖אוּ הַיָּ֑מָּה וַיִּרְדְּפ֨וּ מִצְרַ֜יִם אַחֲרֵ֧י אֲבוֹתֵיכֶ֛ם בְּרֶ֥כֶב וּבְפָרָשִׁ֖ים יַם־סֽוּף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I brought you and your fathers out of Egypt, and you came to the sea: and the Egyptians pursued your fathers with chariots and horsemen, as far as the Red Sea.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vein minä myös teidän isänne Egyptistä ulos, ja te tulitte meren tykö; ja Egyptiläiset ajoivat teidän isiänne takaa vaunuin ja ratsasmiesten kanssa Punaiseen mereen saakka.
verses.king_james: And I brought your fathers out of Egypt: and ye came unto the sea; and the Egyptians pursued after your fathers with chariots and horsemen unto the Red sea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Clamaverunt autem ad Dominum filii Israël : qui posuit tenebras inter vos et Ægyptios, et adduxit super eos mare, et operuit eos. Viderunt oculi vestri cuncta quæ in Ægypto fecerim, et habitastis in solitudine multo tempore :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּצְעֲק֣וּ אֶל־יְהֹוָ֗ה וַיָּ֨שֶׂם מַאֲפֵ֜ל בֵּינֵיכֶ֣ם ׀ וּבֵ֣ין הַמִּצְרִ֗ים וַיָּבֵ֨א עָלָ֤יו אֶת־הַיָּם֙ וַיְכַסֵּ֔הוּ וַתִּרְאֶ֙ינָה֙ עֵינֵיכֶ֔ם אֵ֥ת אֲשֶׁר־עָשִׂ֖יתִי בְּמִצְרָ֑יִם וַתֵּשְׁב֥וּ בַמִּדְבָּ֖ר יָמִ֥ים רַבִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the children of Israel cried to the Lord: and he put darkness between you and the Egyptians, and brought the sea upon them, and covered them. Your eyes saw all that I did in Egypt, and you dwelt in the wilderness a long time.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin huusivat he Herran tykö, ja hän pani pimeyden teidän ja Egyptiläisten vaiheelle, ja saatti meren heidän päällensä, ja peitti heidät. Ja teidän silmänne näkivät, mitä minä tein Egyptissä; ja te asuitte korvessa kauvan aikaa.
verses.king_james: And when they cried unto the Lord, he put darkness between you and the Egyptians, and brought the sea upon them, and covered them; and your eyes have seen what I have done in Egypt: and ye dwelt in the wilderness a long season.

verses.latin_vulgate: et introduxi vos in terram Amorrhæi, qui habitabat trans Jordanem. Cumque pugnarent contra vos, tradidi eos in manus vestras, et possedistis terram eorum, atque interfecistis eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (ואבאה) [וָאָבִ֣יא] אֶתְכֶ֗ם אֶל־אֶ֤רֶץ הָאֱמֹרִי֙ הַיּוֹשֵׁב֙ בְּעֵ֣בֶר הַיַּרְדֵּ֔ן וַיִּֽלָּחֲמ֖וּ אִתְּכֶ֑ם וָאֶתֵּ֨ן אוֹתָ֤ם בְּיֶדְכֶם֙ וַתִּירְשׁ֣וּ אֶת־אַרְצָ֔ם וָאַשְׁמִידֵ֖ם מִפְּנֵיכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I brought you into the land of the Amorrhite, who dwelt beyond the Jordan. And when they fought against you, I delivered them into your hands, and you possessed their land, and slew them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä toin teidät Amorilaisten maahan, jotka asuivat tuolla puolella Jordania; ja kuin he sotivat teitä vastaan, annoin minä heidät teidän käsiinne, että te omistaisitte heidän maansa; ja minä hävitin heitä teidän edestänne.
verses.king_james: And I brought you into the land of the Amorites, which dwelt on the other side Jordan; and they fought with you: and I gave them into your hand, that ye might possess their land; and I destroyed them from before you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexit autem Balac filius Sephor rex Moab, et pugnavit contra Israëlem. Misitque et vocavit Balaam filium Beor, ut malediceret vobis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֨קׇם בָּלָ֤ק בֶּן־צִפּוֹר֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ מוֹאָ֔ב וַיִּלָּ֖חֶם בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיִּשְׁלַ֗ח וַיִּקְרָ֛א לְבִלְעָ֥ם בֶּן־בְּע֖וֹר לְקַלֵּ֥ל אֶתְכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Balac son of Sephor king of Moab arose and fought against Israel. And he sent and called for Balaam son of Beor, to curse you:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin nousi Balak Zipporin poika, Moabin kuningas, ja soti Israelia vastaan; ja hän lähetti ja antoi kutsua Bileamin Beorin pojan kiroomaan teitä.
verses.king_james: Then Balak the son of Zippor, king of Moab, arose and warred against Israel, and sent and called Balaam the son of Beor to curse you:

verses.latin_vulgate: et ego nolui audire eum, sed e contrario per illum benedixi vobis, et liberavi vos de manu ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֥א אָבִ֖יתִי לִשְׁמֹ֣עַ לְבִלְעָ֑ם וַיְבָ֤רֶךְ בָּרוֹךְ֙ אֶתְכֶ֔ם וָאַצִּ֥ל אֶתְכֶ֖ם מִיָּדֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I would not hear him, but on the contrary I blessed you by him, and I delivered you out of his hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta en minä tahtonut kuulla Bileamia; ja hän siunaten siunasi teitä, ja minä vapahdin teitä hänen käsistänsä.
verses.king_james: But I would not hearken unto Balaam; therefore he blessed you still: so I delivered you out of his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Transistisque Jordanem, et venistis ad Jericho. Pugnaveruntque contra vos viri civitatis ejus, Amorrhæus et Pherezæus, et Chananæus, et Hethæus, et Gergezæus, et Hevæus, et Jebusæus : et tradidi illos in manus vestras.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּעַבְר֣וּ אֶת־הַיַּרְדֵּן֮ וַתָּבֹ֣אוּ אֶל־יְרִיחוֹ֒ וַיִּלָּחֲמ֣וּ בָכֶ֣ם בַּעֲלֵֽי־יְ֠רִיח֠וֹ הָאֱמֹרִ֨י וְהַפְּרִזִּ֜י וְהַֽכְּנַעֲנִ֗י וְהַֽחִתִּי֙ וְהַגִּרְגָּשִׁ֔י הַחִוִּ֖י וְהַיְבוּסִ֑י וָאֶתֵּ֥ן אוֹתָ֖ם בְּיֶדְכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you passed over the Jordan, and you came to Jericho. And the men of that city fought against you, the Amorrhite, and the Pherezite, and the Chanaanite, and the Hethite, and the Gergesite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite: and I delivered them into your hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin te tulitte Jordanin ylitse ja jouduitte Jerihoon, sotivat Jerihon asuvaiset teitä vastaan, Amorilaiset ja Pheresiläiset ja Kanaanealaiset ja Hetiläiset ja Girgasilaiset ja Heviläiset ja Jebusilaiset; mutta minä annoin heidät teidän käsiinne,
verses.king_james: And ye went over Jordan, and came unto Jericho: and the men of Jericho fought against you, the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Girgashites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites; and I delivered them into your hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misique ante vos crabrones : et ejeci eos de locis suis, duos reges Amorrhæorum, non in gladio nec in arcu tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶשְׁלַ֤ח לִפְנֵיכֶם֙ אֶת־הַצִּרְעָ֔ה וַתְּגָ֤רֶשׁ אוֹתָם֙ מִפְּנֵיכֶ֔ם שְׁנֵ֖י מַלְכֵ֣י הָאֱמֹרִ֑י לֹ֥א בְחַרְבְּךָ֖ וְלֹ֥א בְקַשְׁתֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I sent before you and I drove them out from their places, the two kings of the Amorrhites, not with thy sword nor with thy bow,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lähetin vapsaiset teidän edellänne, ja ne ajoivat heidät ulos teidän edestänne, kaksi Amorilaisten kuningasta, ei sinun miekkas kautta eikä sinun joutses kautta.
verses.king_james: And I sent the hornet before you, which drave them out from before you, even the two kings of the Amorites; but not with thy sword, nor with thy bow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedique vobis terram, in qua non laborastis, et urbes quas non ædificastis, ut habitaretis in eis : vineas, et oliveta, quæ non plantastis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶתֵּ֨ן לָכֶ֜ם אֶ֣רֶץ ׀ אֲשֶׁ֧ר לֹֽא־יָגַ֣עְתָּ בָּ֗הּ וְעָרִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹֽא־בְנִיתֶ֔ם וַתֵּשְׁב֖וּ בָּהֶ֑ם כְּרָמִ֤ים וְזֵיתִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹֽא־נְטַעְתֶּ֔ם אַתֶּ֖ם אֹכְלִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I gave you a land, in which you had not laboured, and cities to dwell in which you built not, vineyards and oliveyards, which you planted not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja annoin teille maan, jossa ette mitään työtä tehneet, ja kaupungit, joita ette rakentaneet, asuaksenne niissä ja syödäksenne viinapuista ja öljypuista, joita ette istuttaneet.
verses.king_james: And I have given you a land for which ye did not labour, and cities which ye built not, and ye dwell in them; of the vineyards and oliveyards which ye planted not do ye eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo timete Dominum, et servite ei perfecto corde atque verissimo : et auferte deos quibus servierunt patres vestri in Mesopotamia et in Ægypto, ac servite Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֞ה יְר֧אוּ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֛ה וְעִבְד֥וּ אֹת֖וֹ בְּתָמִ֣ים וּבֶאֱמֶ֑ת וְהָסִ֣ירוּ אֶת־אֱלֹהִ֗ים אֲשֶׁר֩ עָבְד֨וּ אֲבוֹתֵיכֶ֜ם בְּעֵ֤בֶר הַנָּהָר֙ וּבְמִצְרַ֔יִם וְעִבְד֖וּ אֶת־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now therefore fear the Lord, and serve him with a perfect and most sincere heart: and put away the gods which your fathers served in Mesopotamia and in Egypt, and serve the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Peljätkäät siis nyt Herraa ja palvelkaat häntä täydellisesti ja uskollisesti, ja hyljätkäät ne jumalat, joita teidän isänne palvelivat tuolla puolella virtaa ja Egyptissä, ja palvelkaat Herraa.
verses.king_james: Now therefore fear the Lord, and serve him in sincerity and in truth: and put away the gods which your fathers served on the other side of the flood, and in Egypt; and serve ye the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sin autem malum vobis videtur ut Domino serviatis, optio vobis datur : eligite hodie quod placet, cui servire potissimum debeatis : utrum diis, quibus servierunt patres vestri in Mesopotamia, an diis Amorrhæorum, in quorum terra habitatis : ego autem et domus mea serviemus Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם֩ רַ֨ע בְּֽעֵינֵיכֶ֜ם לַעֲבֹ֣ד אֶת־יְהֹוָ֗ה בַּחֲר֨וּ לָכֶ֣ם הַיּוֹם֮ אֶת־מִ֣י תַעֲבֹדוּן֒ אִ֣ם אֶת־אֱלֹהִ֞ים אֲשֶׁר־עָבְד֣וּ אֲבוֹתֵיכֶ֗ם אֲשֶׁר֙ (בעבר) [מֵעֵ֣בֶר] הַנָּהָ֔ר וְאִם֙ אֶת־אֱלֹהֵ֣י הָאֱמֹרִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר אַתֶּ֖ם יֹשְׁבִ֣ים בְּאַרְצָ֑ם וְאָנֹכִ֣י וּבֵיתִ֔י נַעֲבֹ֖ד אֶת־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: But if it seem evil to you to serve the Lord, you have your choice: choose this day that which pleaseth you, whom you would rather serve, whether the gods which your fathers served in Mesopotamia, or the gods of the Amorrhites, in whose land you dwell: but as for me and my house we will serve thee Lord,
verses.finnish_biblia: Jollei teidän kelpaa Herraa palvella, niin valitkaat teillenne tänäpäivänä ketä te palvelette, niitä jumalia, joita teidän isänne palvelivat sillä puolella virtaa, eli Amorilaisten jumalia, joiden maassa te asutte. Mutta minä ja minun huoneeni palvelemme Herraa.
verses.king_james: And if it seem evil unto you to serve the Lord, choose you this day whom ye will serve; whether the gods which your fathers served that were on the other side of the flood, or the gods of the Amorites, in whose land ye dwell: but as for me and my house, we will serve the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responditque populus, et ait : Absit a nobis ut relinquamus Dominum, et serviamus diis alienis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַן הָעָם֙ וַיֹּ֔אמֶר חָלִ֣ילָה לָּ֔נוּ מֵעֲזֹ֖ב אֶת־יְהֹוָ֑ה לַעֲבֹ֖ד אֱלֹהִ֥ים אֲחֵרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the people answered, and said, God forbid we should leave the Lord, and serve strange gods.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vastasi kansa ja sanoi: pois se meistä, että me hylkäisimme Herran ja palvelisimme muita jumalia.
verses.king_james: And the people answered and said, God forbid that we should forsake the Lord, to serve other gods;

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus Deus noster, ipse eduxit nos et patres nostros de terra Ægypti, de domo servitutis : fecitque videntibus nobis signa ingentia, et custodivit nos in omni via per quam ambulavimus, et in cunctis populis per quos transivimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֚י יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵ֔ינוּ הוּא֩ הַמַּעֲלֶ֨ה אֹתָ֧נוּ וְאֶת־אֲבוֹתֵ֛ינוּ מֵאֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרַ֖יִם מִבֵּ֣ית עֲבָדִ֑ים וַאֲשֶׁ֧ר עָשָׂ֣ה לְעֵינֵ֗ינוּ אֶת־הָאֹת֤וֹת הַגְּדֹלוֹת֙ הָאֵ֔לֶּה וַֽיִּשְׁמְרֵ֗נוּ בְּכׇל־הַדֶּ֙רֶךְ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הָלַ֣כְנוּ בָ֔הּ וּבְכֹל֙ הָֽעַמִּ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָבַ֖רְנוּ בְּקִרְבָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Lord our God he brought us and our fathers out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage: and did very great signs in our sight, and preserved us in all the way by which we journeyed, and among all the people through whom we passed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Herra meidän Jumalamme on se, joka johdatti meitä ja meidän isiämme Egyptin maalta orjuuden huoneesta, ja joka teki meidän silmäimme edessä niitä suuria tunnusmerkkejä, ja varjeli meitä kaikella matkalla, jota me vaelsimme, ja kaikkein kansain seassa, joiden lävitse me kävimme.
verses.king_james: For the Lord our God, he it is that brought us up and our fathers out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage, and which did those great signs in our sight, and preserved us in all the way wherein we went, and among all the people through whom we passed:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ejecit universas gentes, Amorrhæum habitatorem terræ quam nos intravimus. Serviemus igitur Domino, quia ipse est Deus noster.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְגָ֨רֶשׁ יְהֹוָ֜ה אֶת־כׇּל־הָעַמִּ֗ים וְאֶת־הָאֱמֹרִ֛י יֹשֵׁ֥ב הָאָ֖רֶץ מִפָּנֵ֑ינוּ גַּם־אֲנַ֙חְנוּ֙ נַעֲבֹ֣ד אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּי־ה֖וּא אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he hath cast out all the nations, the Amorrhite the inhabitant of the land into which we are come. Therefore we will serve the Lord, for he is our God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra ajoi meidän edestämme ulos kaiken kansan ja Amorilaiset, jotka asuivat maassa: niin me myös palvelemme Herraa, sillä hän on meidän Jumalamme.
verses.king_james: And the Lord drave out from before us all the people, even the Amorites which dwelt in the land: therefore will we also serve the Lord; for he is our God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Josue ad populum : Non poteritis servire Domino : Deus enim sanctus et fortis æmulator est, nec ignoscet sceleribus vestris atque peccatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁ֜עַ אֶל־הָעָ֗ם לֹ֤א תֽוּכְלוּ֙ לַעֲבֹ֣ד אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּֽי־אֱלֹהִ֥ים קְדֹשִׁ֖ים ה֑וּא אֵל־קַנּ֣וֹא ה֔וּא לֹא־יִשָּׂ֥א לְפִשְׁעֲכֶ֖ם וּלְחַטֹּאותֵיכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joshua said to the people: You will not be able to serve the Lord: for he is a holy God, and mighty and jealous, and will not forgive your wickedness and sins.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josua sanoi kansalle: ette taida palvella Herraa, sillä hän on pyhä Jumala ja hän on kiivas Jumala, joka ei säästä teidän syntejänne.
verses.king_james: And Joshua said unto the people, Ye cannot serve the Lord: for he is an holy God; he is a jealous God; he will not forgive your transgressions nor your sins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si dimiseritis Dominum, et servieritis diis alienis, convertet se, et affliget vos atque subvertet, postquam vobis præstiterit bona.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י תַֽעַזְבוּ֙ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה וַעֲבַדְתֶּ֖ם אֱלֹהֵ֣י נֵכָ֑ר וְשָׁ֨ב וְהֵרַ֤ע לָכֶם֙ וְכִלָּ֣ה אֶתְכֶ֔ם אַחֲרֵ֖י אֲשֶׁר־הֵיטִ֥יב לָכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: If you leave the Lord, and serve strange gods, he will turn, and will afflict you, and will destroy you after all the good he hath done you.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin te hylkäätte Herran ja palvelette muukalaisia jumalia, niin hän kääntää hänensä, ja tekee teille pahaa, ja hukuttaa teitä sen jälkeen, kuin hän teille hyvää teki.
verses.king_james: If ye forsake the Lord, and serve strange gods, then he will turn and do you hurt, and consume you, after that he hath done you good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque populus ad Josue : Nequaquam ita ut loqueris erit, sed Domino serviemus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר הָעָ֖ם אֶל־יְהוֹשֻׁ֑עַ לֹ֕א כִּ֥י אֶת־יְהֹוָ֖ה נַעֲבֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the people said to Joshua: No, it shall not be so as thou sayest, but we will serve the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi kansa Josualle: ei suinkaan, vaan me palvelemme Herraa.
verses.king_james: And the people said unto Joshua, Nay; but we will serve the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Josue ad populum : Testes, inquit, vos estis, quia ipsi elegeritis vobis Dominum ut serviatis ei. Responderuntque : Testes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁ֜עַ אֶל־הָעָ֗ם עֵדִ֤ים אַתֶּם֙ בָּכֶ֔ם כִּי־אַתֶּ֞ם בְּחַרְתֶּ֥ם לָכֶ֛ם אֶת־יְהֹוָ֖ה לַעֲבֹ֣ד אוֹת֑וֹ וַיֹּאמְר֖וּ עֵדִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joshua said to the people, You are witnesses, that you yourselves have chosen you the Lord to serve him. And they answered: We are witnesses.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josua sanoi kansalle: te olette teillenne todistajat, että te valitsitte teillenne Herran, palvellaksenne häntä. Ja he sanoivat: me olemme todistajat.
verses.king_james: And Joshua said unto the people, Ye are witnesses against yourselves that ye have chosen you the Lord, to serve him. And they said, We are witnesses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo, ait, auferte deos alienos de medio vestri, et inclinate corda vestra ad Dominum Deum Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֕ה הָסִ֛ירוּ אֶת־אֱלֹהֵ֥י הַנֵּכָ֖ר אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּקִרְבְּכֶ֑ם וְהַטּוּ֙ אֶת־לְבַבְכֶ֔ם אֶל־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now therefore, said he, put away strange gods from among you, and incline your hearts to the Lord the God of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin pankaat nyt teidän tyköänne muukalaiset jumalat pois, jotka teidän seassanne ovat, ja kumartakaat teidän sydämenne Herran Israelin Jumalan tykö.
verses.king_james: Now therefore put away, said he, the strange gods which are among you, and incline your heart unto the Lord God of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque populus ad Josue : Domino Deo nostro serviemus, et obedientes erimus præceptis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֥וּ הָעָ֖ם אֶל־יְהוֹשֻׁ֑עַ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהֵ֙ינוּ֙ נַעֲבֹ֔ד וּבְקוֹל֖וֹ נִשְׁמָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the people said to Joshua: We will serve the Lord our God, and we will be obedient to his commandments.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kansa sanoi Josualle: me tahdomme palvella Herraa meidän Jumalaamme, ja olemme hänen äänellensä kuuliaiset.
verses.king_james: And the people said unto Joshua, The Lord our God will we serve, and his voice will we obey.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percussit ergo Josue in die illo fœdus, et proposuit populo præcepta atque judicia in Sichem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּכְרֹ֨ת יְהוֹשֻׁ֧עַ בְּרִ֛ית לָעָ֖ם בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֑וּא וַיָּ֥שֶׂם ל֛וֹ חֹ֥ק וּמִשְׁפָּ֖ט בִּשְׁכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Joshua therefore on that day made a covenant, and set before the people commandments and judgments in Sichem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Josua teki sinä päivänä liiton kansan kanssa, ja asetti säädyn ja oikeuden heidän eteensä Sikemissä.
verses.king_james: So Joshua made a covenant with the people that day, and set them a statute and an ordinance in Shechem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scripsit quoque omnia verba hæc in volumine legis Domini : et tulit lapidem pergrandem, posuitque eum subter quercum, quæ erat in sanctuario Domini :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּכְתֹּ֤ב יְהוֹשֻׁ֙עַ֙ אֶת־הַדְּבָרִ֣ים הָאֵ֔לֶּה בְּסֵ֖פֶר תּוֹרַ֣ת אֱלֹהִ֑ים וַיִּקַּח֙ אֶ֣בֶן גְּדוֹלָ֔ה וַיְקִימֶ֣הָ שָּׁ֔ם תַּ֚חַת הָֽאַלָּ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֖ר בְּמִקְדַּ֥שׁ יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he wrote all these things in the volume of the law of the Lord: and he took a great stone, and set it under the oak that was in the sanctuary of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josua kirjoitti kaikki nämä sanat Jumalan lakikirjaan, ja otti suuren kiven, ja pystytti sen siinä tammen alla, joka oli Herran pyhässä.
verses.king_james: And Joshua wrote these words in the book of the law of God, and took a great stone, and set it up there under an oak, that was by the sanctuary of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit ad omnem populum : En lapis iste erit vobis in testimonium, quod audierit omnia verba Domini, quæ locutus est vobis : ne forte postea negare velitis, et mentiri Domino Deo vestro.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁ֜עַ אֶל־כׇּל־הָעָ֗ם הִנֵּ֨ה הָאֶ֤בֶן הַזֹּאת֙ תִּֽהְיֶה־בָּ֣נוּ לְעֵדָ֔ה כִּי־הִ֣יא שָֽׁמְעָ֗ה אֵ֚ת כׇּל־אִמְרֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבֶּ֖ר עִמָּ֑נוּ וְהָיְתָ֤ה בָכֶם֙ לְעֵדָ֔ה פֶּֽן־תְּכַחֲשׁ֖וּן בֵּאלֹהֵיכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to all the people: Behold this stone shall be a testimony unto you, that it hath heard all the words of the Lord, which he hath spoken to you: lest perhaps hereafter you will deny it, and lie to the Lord your God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josua sanoi kaikelle kansalle: katso, tämä kivi pitää oleman todistus meidän vaiheellamme; sillä hän on kuullut kaikki Herran puheet, jotka hän meidän kanssamme puhunut on, ja pitää oleman teille todistus, ettette teidän Jumalaanne kiellä.
verses.king_james: And Joshua said unto all the people, Behold, this stone shall be a witness unto us; for it hath heard all the words of the Lord which he spake unto us: it shall be therefore a witness unto you, lest ye deny your God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dimisitque populum, singulos in possessionem suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְשַׁלַּ֤ח יְהוֹשֻׁ֙עַ֙ אֶת־הָעָ֔ם אִ֖ישׁ לְנַחֲלָתֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he sent the people away every one to their own possession,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin antoi Josua kansan mennä, itsekunkin perintöönsä.
verses.king_james: So Joshua let the people depart, every man unto his inheritance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et post hæc mortuus est Josue filius Nun servus Domini, centum et decem annorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י אַֽחֲרֵי֙ הַדְּבָרִ֣ים הָאֵ֔לֶּה וַיָּ֛מׇת יְהוֹשֻׁ֥עַ בִּן־נ֖וּן עֶ֣בֶד יְהֹוָ֑ה בֶּן־מֵאָ֥ה וָעֶ֖שֶׂר שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And after these things Joshua the son of Nun the servant of the Lord died, being a hundred and ten years old:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sen jälkeen tapahtui, että Josua Nunin poika, Herran palvelia, kuoli, sadan ja kymmenen ajastaikaisena.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after these things, that Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of the Lord, died, being an hundred and ten years old.

verses.latin_vulgate: sepelieruntque eum in finibus possessionis suæ in Thamnathsare, quæ est sita in monte Ephraim, a septentrionali parte montis Gaas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְבְּר֤וּ אֹתוֹ֙ בִּגְב֣וּל נַחֲלָת֔וֹ בְּתִמְנַת־סֶ֖רַח אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּהַר־אֶפְרָ֑יִם מִצְּפ֖וֹן לְהַר־גָּֽעַשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they buried him in the border of his possession in Thamnathsare, which is situate in mount Ephraim, on the north side of mount Gaas.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he hautasivat hänen oman perityn maansa rajoihin, Timnat Serakiin, joka on Ephraimin vuorella, pohjan puolelle Gaasin vuorta.
verses.king_james: And they buried him in the border of his inheritance in Timnath–serah, which is in mount Ephraim, on the north side of the hill of Gaash.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servivitque Israël Domino cunctis diebus Josue et seniorum, qui longo vixerunt tempore post Josue, et qui noverunt omnia opera Domini quæ fecerat in Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲבֹ֤ד יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה כֹּ֖ל יְמֵ֣י יְהוֹשֻׁ֑עַ וְכֹ֣ל ׀ יְמֵ֣י הַזְּקֵנִ֗ים אֲשֶׁ֨ר הֶאֱרִ֤יכוּ יָמִים֙ אַחֲרֵ֣י יְהוֹשֻׁ֔עַ וַאֲשֶׁ֣ר יָדְע֗וּ אֵ֚ת כׇּל־מַעֲשֵׂ֣ה יְהֹוָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשָׂ֖ה לְיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Israel served the Lord all the days of Joshua, and of the ancients that lived a long time after Joshua, and that had known all the works of the Lord which he had done in Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israel palveli Herraa kaikkena Josuan elinaikana, niin myös kaikkena vanhimpain elinaikana, jotka kauvan aikaa josuan perästä elivät ja tiesivät kaikki Herran teot, jotka hän Israelille tehnyt oli.
verses.king_james: And Israel served the Lord all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders that overlived Joshua, and which had known all the works of the Lord, that he had done for Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ossa quoque Joseph, quæ tulerant filii Israël de Ægypto, sepelierunt in Sichem, in parte agri quem emerat Jacob a filiis Hemor patris Sichem, centum novellis ovibus, et fuit in possessionem filiorum Joseph.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־עַצְמ֣וֹת י֠וֹסֵ֠ף אֲשֶׁר־הֶעֱל֨וּ בְנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵ֥ל ׀ מִמִּצְרַ֘יִם֮ קָבְר֣וּ בִשְׁכֶם֒ בְּחֶלְקַ֣ת הַשָּׂדֶ֗ה אֲשֶׁ֨ר קָנָ֧ה יַעֲקֹ֛ב מֵאֵ֛ת בְּנֵי־חֲמ֥וֹר אֲבִֽי־שְׁכֶ֖ם בְּמֵאָ֣ה קְשִׂיטָ֑ה וַיִּֽהְי֥וּ לִבְנֵֽי־יוֹסֵ֖ף לְנַחֲלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the bones of Joseph which the children of Israel had taken out of Egypt, they buried in Sichem, in that part of the field which Jacob had bought of the sons of Hemor the father of Sichem, for a hundred young ewes, and it was in the possession of the sons of Joseph.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josephin luut, jotka Israelin lapset olivat tuoneet Egyptistä, hautasivat he Sikemiin, siihen pellon kappaleeseen, jonka Jakob osti Hemorin Sikemin isän lapsilta sadalla penningillä. Ja se tuli Josephin lasten perinnöksi.
verses.king_james: And the bones of Joseph, which the children of Israel brought up out of Egypt, buried they in Shechem, in a parcel of ground which Jacob bought of the sons of Hamor the father of Shechem for an hundred pieces of silver: and it became the inheritance of the children of Joseph.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eleazar quoque filius Aaron mortuus est : et sepelierunt eum in Gabaath Phinees filii ejus, quæ data est ei in monte Ephraim.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶלְעָזָ֥ר בֶּֽן־אַהֲרֹ֖ן מֵ֑ת וַיִּקְבְּר֣וּ אֹת֗וֹ בְּגִבְעַת֙ פִּֽינְחָ֣ס בְּנ֔וֹ אֲשֶׁ֥ר נִתַּן־ל֖וֹ בְּהַ֥ר אֶפְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Eleazar also the son of Aaron died: and they buried him in Gabaath that belongeth to Phinees his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kuoli myös Eleatsar Aaronin poika, ja he hautasivat hänen Gibeassa, joka oli hänen poikansa Pinehaan oma, joka hänelle Ephraimin vuorella annettu oli.
verses.king_james: And Eleazar the son of Aaron died; and they buried him in a hill that pertained to Phinehas his son, which was given him in mount Ephraim.
